questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: $y-x$ curve at an instant for a wave travelling along $x$ axis on a string is shown. Slope at the point $A$ on the curve, as shown, is $53^{\circ}$.
options: Transverse velocity of the particle at point $A$ is positive if the wave is travelling along positive $x$ axis.
Transverse velocity of the particle at point $A$ is positive if the wave is travelling along negative $x$ axis of the particle at point $A$
Magnitude of transverse velocity of the particle at point $A$ is greater than wave speed.
(D) Magnitude of transverse velocity of the particle at point $A$ is lesser than wave speed.
questions.solution:
As shown in the curve, if wave is moving along -x axis, $\mathrm{V}_{\mathrm{p}}$ is positive.
$\begin{aligned} & \frac{V_p}{V_w}=-\tan \theta \\ & |-\tan \theta| \gt 1 \quad \therefore V_p \gt V_w \end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: For a certain transverse standing wave on a long string, an antinode is formed at $\mathrm{x}=0$ and next to it, a node is formed at $x=0.10 \mathrm{~m}$. the position $y(t)$ of the string particle at $x=0$ is shown in figure.
options: Transverse displacement of the particle at $\mathrm{x}=0.05 \mathrm{~m}$ and $\mathrm{t}=0.05 \mathrm{~s}$ is $-2 \sqrt{2} \mathrm{~cm}$.
Transverse displacement of the particle at $\mathrm{x}=0.04 \mathrm{~m}$ and $\mathrm{t}=0.025 \mathrm{~s}$ is $-2 \sqrt{2} \mathrm{~cm}$.
Speed of the travelling waves that interfere to produce this standing wave is $2 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}$.
The transverse velocity of the string particle at $\mathrm{x}=\frac{1}{15} \mathrm{~m}$ and $\mathrm{t}=0.1 \mathrm{~s}$ is $20 \pi \mathrm{~cm} / \mathrm{s}$
questions.solution:
$\frac{\lambda}{4}=0.1 \Rightarrow \lambda=0.4 \mathrm{~m}$
from graph $\Rightarrow \quad \mathrm{T}=0.2 \mathrm{sec}$. and amplitude of standing 
wave is $2 A=4 \mathrm{~cm}$.
Equation of the standing wave
$\begin{aligned}
& y(x, t)=-2 A \cos \left(\frac{2 \pi}{0.4} x\right) \cdot \sin \left(\frac{2 \pi}{0.2} t\right) \mathrm{cm} \\
& y(x=0.05, \quad t=0.05)=-2 \sqrt{2} \mathrm{~cm} \\
& y(x=0.04, t=0.025)=-2 \sqrt{2} \cos 36^{\circ} \\
& \text { speed }=\frac{\lambda}{T}=2 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{sec} . \\
& V_y=\frac{d y}{d t}=-2 A \times \frac{2 \pi}{0.2} \cos \left(\frac{2 \pi x}{0.4}\right) \cdot \cos \left(\frac{2 \pi t}{0.2}\right) \\
& V_y=\left(x=\frac{1}{15} m, t=0.1\right)=20 \pi \quad \mathrm{~cm} / \mathrm{sec} .
\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A car moves towards a hill with speed $v_0$. It blows a horn of frequency $f$ which is heard by an observer following the car with speed $\mathrm{v}_0$. The speed of sound in air is v .
options: The wavelength of sound reaching the hill is $\frac{v}{f}$
The wavelength of sound reaching the hill is $\frac{v-v_c}{f}$
The wavelength of sound of horn directly reaching the observer is $\frac{v+v_c}{f}$
The beat frequency observed by the observer is $\frac{2 v_c\left(v+v_0\right) f}{v^2-v_c^2}$
questions.solution:
Frequency of horn directly heard by observer $\frac{v+v_0}{v+v_c} f$
Frequency of echo $=\frac{v}{v+v_c} f$
Frequency of echo of horn as heard by observer. 
$\frac{v}{v-v_c} f \cdot\left(\frac{v+v_0}{v}\right)$

Frquency of Beats :
$=\left(v+v_0\right) f\left\{\frac{1}{v-v_c}-\frac{1}{v+v_c}\right\}=\frac{2 v_c\left(v+v_0\right)}{\left(v^2-v_c^2\right)} f$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
The vibrations of a string of length 600 cm fixed at both ends are represented by the equation
$y=4 \sin \left(\pi \frac{x}{15}\right) \cos (96 \pi t)$
Where x and y are in cm and t in seconds
options: The maximum displacement of a point $\mathrm{x}=5 \mathrm{~cm}$ is $2 \sqrt{3} \mathrm{~cm}$.
The nodes located along the string are 15 n where integer n varies from 0 to 40 .
The velocity of the particle at $\mathrm{x}=7.5 \mathrm{~cm}$ at $\mathrm{t}=0.25 \mathrm{sec}$ is 0

The equation of the component waves whose superposition gives the above wave are
$2 \sin 2 \pi\left(\frac{x}{30}+48 t\right), 2 \sin 2 \pi\left(\frac{x}{30}-48 t\right)$
questions.solution:
(A) Displacement $=4 \operatorname{Sin}\left(\pi \frac{5}{15}\right)=4 \operatorname{Sin} \frac{\pi}{5}=2 \sqrt{3} \mathrm{Cm}$
(B) $\quad K x=\frac{2 \pi}{\lambda} x=\frac{\pi x}{15} \Rightarrow \lambda=30$

Nodes wil form at $0, \frac{\lambda}{2}, \frac{2 \lambda}{2}, \frac{3 \lambda}{2}, \frac{4 \lambda}{2}, \frac{5 \lambda}{2} \ldots .0,15,30,45 \ldots$ ie 15 is where $\mathrm{n}=0$ to 40
(C) $\quad V=\frac{d y}{d t}=-4 \sin \left(\frac{\pi x}{15}\right)(96 \pi) \sin (96 \pi t)$ at $x=7.5 \mathrm{~m} ; t=\frac{1}{4} w$
$v=-4 \sin \left(\frac{\lambda \times 75}{15}\right)(96 \pi) \sin \left(96 \pi \times \frac{1}{4}\right)=0$
(D) $2 \operatorname{Sin}\left(\frac{\pi x}{15}+96 \pi t\right), 2 \operatorname{Sin}\left(\frac{\pi x}{15}-T 6 \pi t\right)$
$2 \operatorname{Sin} 2 \pi\left(\frac{x}{30}+48 t\right), 2 \operatorname{Sin} 2 \pi\left(\frac{x}{30}-38 t\right)$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Shape of string transmitting wave along $x$-axis at some instant is shown. Velocity of point $P$ is $v=4 \pi \mathrm{~cm} /$ s and $\theta=\tan ^{-1}(0.004 \pi)$
options: Amplitude of wave is 2 mm
Velocity of wave is $10 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}$
Max acceleration of particle is $80 \pi^2 \mathrm{~cm} / \mathrm{sec}^2$
Wave is travelling in -ve x -direction
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned}
& \lambda=1 \mathrm{~m} \\
& v_{\max }=A \omega=4 \pi \\
& \therefore \quad v=\frac{\partial y / \partial \mathrm{t}}{\partial y / \partial x}=\frac{4 \pi \times 10^{-2}}{4 \pi \times 10^{-3}}=10 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s} \\
& A=2 \times 10^{-3} \mathrm{~m} \text { also } v=\frac{v}{\lambda}=10 \mathrm{~Hz} \text { and } a_{\max }=A \omega^2=80 \pi \mathrm{~cm} / \mathrm{s}^2
\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A source of sound of single frequency $v_0$ flies along a straight line which is at a finite distance from the observer. Then the observer hears
options: a frequency $v_0$ at the instant when the source is nearest to him
a frequency greater than $v_0$ the instant when the source is nearest to him
a frequency $v_0$ at an instant later than the instant of nearest position of the source
the variation of frequency with time is linear.
questions.solution: When source is nearest than frequency heard. $f^{\prime}=\frac{f C}{C-V_s \cos \theta}$ after crossing this point frequency obtained corresponding to wave emitted from this point is $\mathrm{f}^{\prime}=\mathrm{f}_0$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Equation of a travelling wave on string is givne by $y=1(m \mathrm{~m}) \sin (t-x)$. An ant is moving on string with constant speed of $\sqrt{2} \mathrm{~mm} / \mathrm{sec}$. with respect to string. Ifat $t=0$ ant is at $x=0$. Then mark correct statements: ( $x$ is in mm and $t$ is in second)
options: At $\mathrm{t}=0$ velocity of ant with respect to ground is $1 \hat{\mathrm{i}} \mathrm{m} / \mathrm{sec}$.
At $\mathrm{t}=0$ velocity of ant with respect to ground is $(2 \hat{i}+\hat{j}) \mathrm{m} / \mathrm{sec}$.
If $t_{\mathrm{a}}$ and t , are the time taken by ant and distubance at $\mathrm{x}=0$ respectively to reach a point $\left(\mathrm{x}_0, 0\right)$ then $t_a \lt t$
If $t_{\mathrm{a}}$ and $t$, are the time taken by ant and distubance at $x=0$ respectively to reach a point ( $\mathrm{x}_0, 0$ ) then $\mathrm{t}_{\mathrm{a}}=\mathrm{t}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Two trains move in the same direction on two close tracks. The train A sounds a horn of single frequency of 1 kHz . At a certain instant of time, when both trains move at the same speed of $36 \mathrm{~km} / \mathrm{hr}$, the two straight tracks deviate at an angle of $60^{\circ}$ with each other. Velocity of sound in air is $300 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}$. Which of the following statement(s) is/are true?
options: The apparent frequency heard by a passenger on the second train $B$ just after he passes the bend will be 1.05 kHz .
The apparent frequency heard by a passenger on the first train $A$, just after the second train $B$ passes the bend will be 1 kHz .
The apparent frequency heard by a passenger on the second train $B$ just after he passes the bend will be 983 Hz .

If the first train A , that produces the sound, were to pass the bend while the other train B goes on the straight track then the apparent frequency heard by a passenger on the latter train $B$ will be less than 1 kHz .
questions.solution:
At the bend, when the second train B passes the bend and moves at $60^{\circ}$ to the first train $A$.
The apparent frequency heard by the passenger on train B , is given by $n_a=\left[\frac{V-u_B}{V+u_A \cos 60^{\circ}}\right] n$ Given $V=300 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}, u_A=u_B=36 \mathrm{~km} / \mathrm{h}=10 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}$. Thus $n_a=\left[\frac{300-10}{300+5}\right] 1 \mathrm{kHz}=\left(\frac{290}{305}\right)(1000)=950.82 \mathrm{~Hz}$ The passenger on train A is in the same train as source and so will always hear the source frequency of 1 kHz . If the train $A$ turns on the bend on the second track while the passenger on train $B$ hears the sound while moving straight on the first track, the apparent frequency heard by him will be
$n_a=\left[\frac{V-u_B \cos 60^{\circ}}{V+u_A}\right] n=\left[\frac{300-5}{300+10}\right] k H z=\left(\frac{305}{310}\right) k H z=983.87 \mathrm{~Hz} \lt 1 \mathrm{kHz}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A source is moving across a circle given by the equation $x^2+y^2=R^2$, with constant speed $\frac{330 \pi}{6 \sqrt{3}} \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}$, in anti-clockwise sense. A detector is at rest at point ( $2 \mathrm{R}, 0$ ) w.r.t. the centre of the circle. If the frequency emitted by the source is $f$ and the speed of sound, $c=330 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}$. Then
options: the position of the source when the detector records the maximum frequency is $\left(+\frac{\sqrt{3}}{2} R,-\frac{R}{2}\right)$
the coordinate of the source when the detector records minimum frequency is $(0, R)$
the minimum frequency recorded by the detector is $\frac{6 \sqrt{3}}{\pi+6 \sqrt{3}} \mathrm{f}$
the maximum frequency recorded by the detector is $\frac{6 \sqrt{3}}{6 \sqrt{3}-\pi} \mathrm{f}$
questions.solution: Sound emitted by source at A will result in maximum frequency while sound emitted by source at $B$ will result in minimum frequency. Time taken by sound to reach from A to detector $=\frac{2 R \sin 60^{\circ}}{330}$ $\begin{aligned} & \text { Angle travelled by source by this }=\frac{330 \pi}{6 \sqrt{3} R} \times \frac{2 R \sin 60^{\circ}}{330}=\frac{\pi}{6} \\ & f_{\max }=\left(\frac{330}{330-\frac{330 \pi}{6 \sqrt{3}}}\right) f ; f_{\min }=\left(\frac{330}{330+\frac{330 \pi}{6 \sqrt{3}}}\right) \mathrm{f} \end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
A pulse is started at a time $t=0$ along the $+x$ direction on a long, taut string. The shape of the pulse at $t=0$ is given by function $f(x)$ with
$f(x)=\left\{\begin{array}{ccc}\frac{x}{4}+1 & \text { for } & -4 \lt x \leq 0 \\ -x+1 & \text { for } & 0 \lt x \lt 1 \\ 0 & & \text { otherwise }\end{array}\right.$
here $f$ and $x$ are in centimeters. The linear mass density of the string is $50 \mathrm{~g} / \mathrm{m}$ and it is under a tension of 5 N .
options: The shape of the string is drawn at $t=0$ then the area of the pulse enclosed by the string and the x -axis is $2.5 \mathrm{~cm}^2$.
The shape of the string is drawn at $\mathrm{t}=0$ then the area of the pulse enclosed by the string and the x -axis is $5 \mathrm{~cm}^2$.
The transverse velocity of the particle at $x=13 \mathrm{~cm}$ and $\mathrm{t}=0.015 \mathrm{~s}$ will be $-250 \mathrm{~cm} / \mathrm{s}$
The transverse velocity of the particle at $\mathrm{x}=13 \mathrm{~cm}$ and $\mathrm{t}=0.015 \mathrm{~s}$ will be $250 \mathrm{~cm} / \mathrm{s}$
questions.solution:
The shape of the string will be
Area $=\frac{1}{2} \times 5 \times 1=2.5 \mathrm{~cm}^2$ $\text { Wave velocity }=5 \sqrt{\frac{5 \times 1000}{50}} \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}=10 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}$ Thus the part with slope $\frac{1}{4}$ will be present at $x=13 \& t=0.015$. $v_P=\frac{1}{4} \frac{d x}{d t}=\frac{-1}{4} v_{\text {wave }}=-250 \mathrm{~cm} / \mathrm{sec}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: The particle displacement of a travelling longitudinal wave is represented by $S=f(x, t)$. The midpoints of a compression zone and an adjacent rarefaction zone are represented by the letter ' $C$ ' and ' $R$ '. Which of the following is true?
options: $\left|\frac{\partial S}{\partial \mathbf{x}}\right|_C=\left|\frac{\partial S}{\partial \mathbf{x}}\right|_R$
$\left|\frac{\partial \mathrm{S}}{\partial \mathrm{t}}\right|_{\mathrm{C}}=\left|\frac{\partial \mathrm{S}}{\partial \mathrm{t}}\right|_{\mathrm{R}}=0$
(pressure $_{\mathrm{c}}-(\text { pressure })_R=2\left|\frac{\partial \mathrm{~S}}{\partial \mathrm{x}}\right|_{\mathrm{C}} \cdot($ bulk modulus of air $)$
Particles of air are stationary mid-way between ' $C$ ' and ' $R$ '.
questions.solution: $P_C-P_R=2 B A K \quad S=A \sin (k x-\omega t)$ $\left(\frac{\partial \mathrm{S}}{\partial \mathrm{x}}\right)_{\mathrm{C}}=\mathrm{AK}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Shape of string transmitting wave along $x$ axis at some instant is shown Velocity of point $P$ is $v=4 \pi \mathrm{~cm} / \mathrm{s} \square$ and $\theta=\tan ^{-1}(0.004 \pi)$
options: Amplitude of wave is 2 mm
Velocity of wave is $10 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}$
Max acceleration of particle is $80 \pi^2$ $\mathrm{cm} / \mathrm{sec}^2$
Wave is traveling in -ve x-direction

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Consider a travelling simple harmonic wave on a string of mass per unit length $\mu$ and tension T . Kinetic energy per unit length is given by $u_k=\frac{1}{2} \mu\left(\frac{\partial y}{\partial t}\right)^2$ and potential energy per unit length is given by $u_p=\frac{1}{2} T\left(\frac{\partial y}{\partial x}\right)^2$. Mark the CORRECT option(s).
options: Power transmitted by wave equals $\left(\mathrm{u}_{\mathrm{k}}+\mathrm{u}_{\mathrm{p}}\right) \sqrt{\frac{T}{\mu}}$.
$u_k$ and $u_p$ simultaneously attain their maximum and minimum values.
Total energy per unit length of a string is constant when a harmonic wave travels on it.
A small part of string has maximum potential energy when it is at its equilibrium position.
questions.solution: Power $=\frac{d E}{d t} \Rightarrow \frac{\left(u_p+u_K\right) d l}{d t}=\left(u_p+u_k\right) \sqrt{\frac{T}{\mu}}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A sinusoidal wave produced in a stretched string gets reflected and loses $36 \%$ of its energy in getting reflected from the fixed end of the string. Choose the correct statement from the
options: $\frac{\text { Amplitude of reflected wave }}{\text { Amplitude of incident wave }}=\frac{8}{10}$
$\text { Phase difference between incident and reflected wave is } \pi \text {. }$
$\frac{\text { Frequency of reflected wave }}{\text { Frequency of incident wave }}=\frac{4}{5}$
$\text { None of the above. }$
questions.solution:
Let the incident wave has intensity $=1$
So, intensity of reflected wave $=0.64 \mathrm{I}$
$I \propto A_i^2$

And. $0.641 \propto A_k^?$
$\begin{aligned}
& \text { So, }\left(\frac{A_i}{A_8}\right)^2=\left(\frac{1}{0.64}\right) \\
& \Rightarrow \frac{A_2}{A_i}=\frac{4}{5}
\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A standing wave of time period $T$ is set up in a string clamped between two rigid supports. At $\mathrm{t}=0$ antinode is at its maximum displacement 2 A .
options: The energy density of a node is equal to energy density of an antinode for the first time at $\mathrm{t}=\mathrm{T} / 4$.
The energy density of node and antinode becomes equal after $\mathrm{T} / 2$ second.
The displacement of the particle at antinode at $t=\frac{T}{8}$ is $\sqrt{2} A$
The displacement of the particle at node is zero
questions.solution:
Equation of $S H M$ of particle who is at antinode is $\mathrm{y}=2 \mathrm{~A} \sin \left(\frac{2 \pi}{T}\right) t$ at time $\mathrm{t}=\frac{T}{8}$
$\mathrm{y}=2 \mathrm{~A} \sin \frac{\pi}{4}=\sqrt{2} A$
Displacement of particle at note is always zero.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
Consider a source of sound S , and an observer P . The source emits sound of frequency $\mathrm{N}_0$. The frequency observed by P is found to be:
$N_1$ if $P$ approaches $S$ at speed $v$ and $S$ is stationary,
$\mathrm{N}_2$ if S approaches P at speed v and P is stationary, and
$\mathrm{N}_3$ if each of P and S have speeds $\mathrm{v} / 2$ towards one another. Then,
options: $\mathrm{N}_1 \lt \mathrm{N}_2$
$\mathrm{N}_3\gt\mathrm{N}_0$
$N_3 \text { lies between } N_1 \text { and } N_2$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
